reasoning: As of late October 2025, Johannes Kaiser is consistently polling in 4th or 5th place, with support ranging from 5% to roughly 13.4%. The leading candidates, Jeannette Jara and José Antonio Kast, are consistently polling in the 22% to 26% range. Although the frontrunners saw some decline in late October polls, Kaiser remains significantly behind and would need a massive, unprecedented shift in voter sentiment to overtake multiple candidates and secure a plurality in the first round scheduled for November 16, 2025. No major withdrawals or consolidations in his favor have been reported; instead, he continues to split the right-wing vote with Kast and Evelyn Matthei. Consequently, his chances of winning the first round are extremely low.
